app_info.subtitle: Inspect trading repo
app_info.description: Systematic Trading Framework gives ChatGPT repository-scoped development access to a private systematic-trading research project. It supports bounded inspection and Git review, atomic file and unified-diff edits, safe upload import, repository path management, and explicitly confirmed command or Python execution without a static script allowlist.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches repository paths and text matches and returns result identifiers without modifying files or state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Operates only on the mounted private repository and does not write to public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads one repository file by identifier and returns its contents without changing repository state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Retrieves data only from the mounted private repository and does not publish or update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists repository directory entries and metadata without creating, updating, or deleting files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only the mounted private repository and does not communicate changes to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads bounded text content from a repository-relative file path without modifying the file.
justifications.open_world_justification: Retrieves local repository content only and does not publish data to public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Finds repository paths matching a glob-style pattern without changing files or metadata.
justifications.open_world_justification: Searches only the mounted private repository and does not update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches text content across repository files and returns matching lines without modifying files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only local repository content and does not publish or mutate external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Parses repository files to find Python symbols and Markdown headings without changing source files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Operates only on local repository files and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches repository text for references to a symbol and returns locations without modifying files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Uses only local repository content and does not publish or update third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads a bounded repository tree listing without creating, updating, or deleting files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only the mounted private repository and does not communicate state changes externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs a read-only git status command and returns working tree state without changing git data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Inspects only the local git repository and does not push, publish, or contact external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs a read-only git diff command and returns patch text without modifying repository state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Inspects only the local git repository and does not push, publish, or update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs a read-only git log command and returns commit metadata without changing git state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only local repository history and does not publish or update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs a read-only git branch query and returns the current branch name without changing git state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Inspects only the local git repository and does not contact or update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Discovers experiment artifact directories and metadata without modifying artifacts or logs.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only repository-local experiment outputs and does not publish data externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads one experiment artifact such as JSON, CSV, or text output without changing the artifact.
justifications.open_world_justification: Retrieves local repository artifacts only and does not update public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Opens the requested Optuna SQLite database in read-only mode and returns study and trial metadata.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads a repository-local database file and does not send updates to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads and parses repository configuration files without changing configuration or runtime defaults.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only mounted repository files and does not publish or mutate external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads a bounded tail from a repository log file without modifying logs or runtime state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Retrieves local log content only and does not publish or update external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Atomically creates or replaces one UTF-8 repository file and returns prior/resulting hashes for optimistic concurrency.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only below the canonical mounted repository root and does not update third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Normal source edits are local and hash-guardable; protected paths, root escapes, and .git writes are rejected.

justifications.read_only_justification: Checks or atomically applies a multi-file unified diff and may create, update, or delete repository files.
justifications.open_world_justification: Applies changes only inside the canonical mounted repository root.
justifications.destructive_justification: A patch may delete files, so deletion hunks require explicit non-empty confirmation and the complete patch must pass before mutation.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates repository directories recursively.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates directories only below the canonical mounted repository root.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not overwrite or delete existing files.

justifications.read_only_justification: Moves or renames a repository file or directory.
justifications.open_world_justification: Both source and destination remain inside the canonical mounted repository root.
justifications.destructive_justification: Overwrite mode can replace a destination and therefore requires explicit non-empty confirmation.

justifications.read_only_justification: Deletes a repository file or an explicitly recursive repository directory.
justifications.open_world_justification: Deletes only below the canonical mounted repository root.
justifications.destructive_justification: Deletion always requires explicit non-empty confirmation and refuses the repository root, .git, and protected secret-like paths.

justifications.read_only_justification: Atomically copies a regular uploaded file into the repository and returns source and destination hashes.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads only configured upload roots such as /mnt/data and writes only inside the mounted repository.
justifications.destructive_justification: Overwrite mode can replace a destination and therefore requires explicit non-empty confirmation.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs an arbitrary argument-array development command that may create or modify repository artifacts.
justifications.open_world_justification: Runs as the non-root MCP container user with a working directory constrained to the mounted repository.
justifications.destructive_justification: Arbitrary execution can mutate repository state and therefore requires explicit non-empty confirmation on every call.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs inline Python or any repository Python file without a static allowlist and may write repository artifacts.
justifications.open_world_justification: Repository scripts execute as the non-root MCP container user with a repository-constrained working directory.
justifications.destructive_justification: Python execution can mutate repository state and therefore requires explicit non-empty confirmation on every call.

justifications.read_only_justification: Legacy compatibility tool that runs only allowlisted repository Python scripts after explicit confirmation; new clients should use run_python.
justifications.open_world_justification: Execution is limited to local repository scripts and is not designed to publish public internet state or update third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: The tool does not expose delete, overwrite, credential revocation, or irreversible admin operations, and execution is restricted by an allowlist and confirmation token.

test_cases.description: Inspect the repository structure before answering an architecture question.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Systematic Trading Framework to show me the top-level project structure and identify where experiments, features, and tests live.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a concise repository tree summary and identifies the main directories relevant to experiments, features, and tests.

test_cases.description: Read and summarize a repository file.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Systematic Trading Framework to read README.md and summarize the project purpose and quality rules.
test_cases.expected_output: Summarizes the repository purpose, workflow, and documented quality rules from README.md.

test_cases.description: Search code for a function or class and find references.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Systematic Trading Framework to find where run_experiment is defined and where it is referenced.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the symbol definition and relevant reference locations with file paths and line numbers.

test_cases.description: Inspect current git state before discussing local changes.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Systematic Trading Framework to check the current branch and summarize the working tree changes.
test_cases.expected_output: Reports the active branch and a concise summary of modified and untracked repository paths.

test_cases.description: Review experiment artifacts and related configuration.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Systematic Trading Framework to list recent experiment runs, then read the summary artifact for one available run.
test_cases.expected_output: Lists available experiment runs and summarizes the selected run artifact without modifying any files.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for live trading execution requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Place a live market order for EURUSD right now.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it does not provide live trading or broker order execution tools.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated personal productivity requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Add a meeting to my calendar for tomorrow morning.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because calendar management is outside the repository inspection workflow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for requests to browse public market news.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Search the web for today's latest Federal Reserve news and summarize it.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it only exposes the local repository and does not provide web browsing or current news search.
